game.name: Boyz Weekend 2026
game.start_location: Zephyr Hotel, 250 Beach St, San Francisco, CA 94133
game.end_location: Buena Vista Bar, 2765 Hyde St, San Francisco, CA 94109

clues.title: Gatekeeper — Gold Rush Infrastructure
clues.instructions: This city did not expand — it accumulated. Stand where water once stood. Find physical evidence that the shoreline moved. Send: (1) photo of evidence, (2) name one ship permanently buried beneath San Francisco.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if submission includes physical shoreline-change evidence and names a historically buried ship beneath San Francisco. Optional hint costs 1 point: 'You are looking for a seam or change in material.'

clues.title: Fire Forced Order
clues.instructions: Twice the city burned. After that, improvisation ended. Find a nearby structure that reflects what changed. Send: (1) photo, (2) the material that became standard — and why.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if submission identifies a relevant structure and explains the standard material adopted and the reason tied to post-fire rebuilding.

clues.title: Federal Concrete
clues.instructions: This shoreline was redesigned when unemployment was the crisis. Identify one structure here funded by Washington, not the city. Send: (1) photo, (2) what WPA stands for.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if structure is identified as federally funded and answer includes correct WPA expansion.

clues.title: A Woman With a Ladder
clues.instructions: She admired firefighters enough to leave money behind. From here, identify the structure she funded and what it honors. Send: photo plus dedication purpose.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if submission identifies the funded structure and clearly states what dedication it honors.

clues.title: Before Bridges
clues.instructions: One athletic institution here predates the Golden Gate Bridge by more than sixty years. Name it and give its founding year.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer identifies South End Rowing Club and founding year 1873.

clues.title: Strategy in Steel
clues.instructions: This vessel represents a wartime strategy: Build many. Build fast. Win by repetition. Identify the ship and the war.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer identifies SS Jeremiah O'Brien and World War II.

clues.title: Silent Pacific
clues.instructions: Steel, narrow corridors, Pacific risk. Identify the submarine and the ocean theater.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer identifies USS Pampanito and Pacific theater.

clues.title: Waymo (Required)
clues.instructions: For a moment, remove yourselves from control. Request a Waymo from Hyde Street to 1083 Lombard Street. Inside the vehicle, send: (1) a photo, (2) complete this sentence: 'Even without a driver, San Francisco still ______.'
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if team submits in-vehicle proof and a completed sentence response.

clues.title: Chosen Inconvenience
clues.instructions: This street is engineered for spectacle. This address is engineered for living. Stand at 1083 Lombard. Send a 15-second video explaining: one sacrifice, one advantage.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if 15-second video includes both one sacrifice and one advantage while at 1083 Lombard.

clues.title: Democracy Saved It
clues.instructions: In 1947, voters chose inconvenience over efficiency. Board the Powell–Hyde cable car downhill. While moving, send a video: 'We earned the descent.' Then proceed to Buena Vista.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if team submits moving cable car video with required phrase.

clues.title: Final Clue A — Improvisation & Geography
clues.instructions: Buena Vista final table round (delivered in person). In the 1960s, one San Francisco band became defined by never playing the same song the same way twice. Name the neighborhood most associated with that movement — and the band.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer is Haight-Ashbury and Grateful Dead.

clues.title: Final Clue B — Succession & Shock
clues.instructions: Buena Vista final table round (delivered in person). In 1978, two assassinations changed San Francisco politics. Who became mayor as a result — and later served in the U.S. Senate for decades?
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer is Dianne Feinstein.

clues.title: Bonus Clue C — Finish Line Round 1
clues.instructions: Final stretch question at the finish venue. Provide your best team answer when prompted.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer matches the event host's expected response.

clues.title: Bonus Clue D — Finish Line Round 2
clues.instructions: Final stretch question at the finish venue. Submit your final team response.
clues.ai_rubric: PASS if answer matches the event host's expected response.

sabotage_catalog.name: Map Scramble
sabotage_catalog.description: Scramble the target team's clue map for 3 minutes (display a fake hint overlay).

sabotage_catalog.name: Time Bomb
sabotage_catalog.description: Force the target team to wait 60 seconds before they can submit their next clue.

sabotage_catalog.name: Double or Nothing
sabotage_catalog.description: Bet 30 points on your own next clue — PASS verdict doubles the payout; FAIL costs you 30 extra.

sabotage_catalog.name: Clue Peek
sabotage_catalog.description: Reveal the title (but not text) of your team's next clue immediately.

sabotage_catalog.name: Saboteur's Taunt
sabotage_catalog.description: Send an anonymous trash-talk message to a target team's event feed.
